title: Born visually impaired, he is now opening the world of books for others
content: Pon Sakthivel, a visually impaired Tamil teacher from Tamil Nadu, is helping blind students access education by digitising printed books into accessible formats. Through technology, a readers’ club and his passion for learning, he is opening the world of books to visually challenged readers.

title: ‘Operation Tiger’ triggers panic in Sena (UBT) as six MPs skip emergency Delhi meet
content: Speculation of a fresh rebellion within Shiv Sena (UBT) intensified after six Lok Sabha MPs skipped a crucial parliamentary party meeting in New Delhi. The party had issued a mandatory whip, and the absence of the MPs has fueled rumours of a possible switch to the Eknath Shinde-led Shiv Sena faction. Party leaders have warned of disciplinary and legal action, while Congress has accused the BJP of engineering defections for political gain. The political battle over the alleged “Operation Tiger” has now sparked a major showdown in Maharashtra politics.

title: “No student should ever feel the desire to end their life”: Rahul Gandhi
content: Rahul Gandhi criticised India’s education system, saying it places excessive stress on students and functions as a “rejection system” rather than a selection system. He also raised concerns about exam pressure, coaching culture, student mental health and employment-linked education.

title: Telegram challenge puts spotlight on Section 69A of the IT Act
content: Using the provision to block an entire platform may infringe on Constitutional rights to freedom of expression and freedom to practice a profession or run a business, similar to a blanket ban on internet services

title: Indian seafarer dies after days without medical help aboard oil tanker
content: Nishanth Uirthanathan, a 35-year-old Indian seafarer from Thoothukudi, died aboard the MT Celestial Sea after days of illness and delayed medical assistance. His death has raised serious concerns about maritime safety, crew welfare, emergency response systems and the conditions faced by seafarers operating in high-risk waters.

title: AP JAC Amaravati demands early constitution of 12th PRC
content: Leaders call upon government employees to be prepared for an agitation to secure their long-pending service and financial demands

title: Congress’s victory and 11 additional votes bolster image of DKS in his first test
content: The Congress held its fort tightly, and the election preparation seems to have been meticulously planned and executed

title: SUCC urges Governor to order a probe into alleged irregularities at Sanskrit varsity
content: The letter submitted to the Governor alleges irregularities in the use of funds, faculty appointments, admissions to PhD and other programmes, and the construction of the student amenities centre and auditorium on the main campus

title: ₹6,000 cr. renewable energy project among ₹30,515 cr. proposals cleared by SIPB
content: Investment proposals received from 50 companies across diverse sectors which are expected to generate an estimated 29,677 jobs in Andhra Pradesh

title: Jagan visits Sai Krishna’s family, demands CBI probe into alleged custodial death
content: YSRCP chief alleges complaints submitted by the victim’s family were ignored by authorities; calls for stringent action against suspended Circle Inspector

title: Watch: U.S.-Iran peace deal signed: does Iran come out on top? | Above the Fold | 18.06.2026
content: A new U.S.-Iran peace deal, a politically significant Governor’s address in Tamil Nadu, and a courtroom battle over West Bengal’s Opposition leadership — here are the stories shaping the day.

title: Kerala Administrative Tribunal issues interim stay of two weeks on transfer of DHS
content: Principal Bench of KAT in Thiruvananthapuram issues an interim stay on the transfer on a petition filed by DHS K.J. Reena.  She approached the tribunal, pointing out the discrepancies in the GO transferring her and stating out that she had been forced into an ignominious exit by the government, that too by raising a baseless allegation against her

title: Lokayukta notice to five Bagalkot officials over pending relief for land acquisition
content: The officers of the Bagalkot Town Development Authority asked to produce relevant documents, among other things

title: Rain brings respite as temperatures plunge across Vizag
content: Downpour began on Wednesday night and continued intermittently through Thursday leading to traffic snarls due to waterlogging in some low-lying areas; the IMD has forecast light to moderate rain or thundershowers at a few places in North Coastal Andhra for the next three days

title: A.P.’s rainfall deficit rises as rain plays hide and seek
content: Twenty districts have reported either deficient or large deficient rainfall with deficit crossing 60% in Polavaram, ASR, Konaseema and Annamayya districts

title: Kerala govt. should explain reason for change of stance on PM SHRI: Pinarayi
content: He refutes Chief Minister’s  claim that the State government had received funds as part of PM SHRI, adding  the former LDF government signed MoU as the Union government had withheld the Samagra Shiksha Scheme funds, disrupting important spending in the education sector.  Signing of MoU does not bind the State to implement the scheme. It can be implemented only if the UDF government takes a policy decision to that effect

title: Naidu to launch induction stove distribution for Anganwadis in Palnadu on June 20
content: Chief Minister Chandrababu Naidu will also participate in Annadata Sukhibhava and Swachha Andhra-Swarna Andhra programmes during the visit and interact with a tenant farmer who made a mark in natural farming

title: Assault on minor: case against old-age home manager, staff
content: A 17-year-old boy, who was staying at the institution in Omalloor, complained to Childline about the alleged abuse.

title: 'You'll be criticised for captaincy as well': Gavaskar's candid verdict on Gill
content: Shubman Gill's exceptional batting form continued with a magnificent 154, leading India to an ODI series win against Afghanistan. His stellar performance, coupled with a strong showing from young pacers like Gurnoor Brar, earned praise from Sunil Gavaskar, who highlighted the positive impact of personal form on captaincy.

title: 'Look within': INDIA bloc ally hits back as Congress cries 'dhoka' after RS setback
content: CPI(ML) Liberation has denied allegations of cross-voting in the Jharkhand Rajya Sabha election, stating both its MLAs voted for the Congress candidate as decided. The party criticized the Congress for failing to secure its own legislators' support and warned that blaming allies could harm the INDIA bloc.

title: How Sundar Pichai 'cheered up' staff when ex-Microsoft CEO said Chrome 'a rounding error'
content: Google CEO Sundar Pichai revealed how he motivated the Chrome team after Microsoft's ex-CEO Steve Ballmer dismissed the browser as a "rounding error." Pichai used the criticism to inspire his team, emphasizing that aggressive iteration and ambitious goals led to Chrome's eventual dominance as the world's most-used browser.

title: 'Mere 70 runs kam kar diye': Ishan Kishan's playful dig at Shubman Gill
content: India achieved a dominant 170-run win against Afghanistan in the second ODI. Shubman Gill and Ishan Kishan scored centuries, forming a significant partnership. This victory gives India an unassailable 2-0 lead in the three-match series. The focus now shifts to the final ODI as India aims for a clean sweep.

title: Armed with new sub, Pak mulls presence in Bay of Bengal for first time since 1971
content: Pakistan is signaling a sustained naval presence in the Bay of Bengal with its new, advanced submarine, PNS Hangor. This move, coupled with warming ties with Bangladesh, marks a significant shift in Pakistan's maritime strategy. The technologically superior submarine, built in China, enhances Pakistan's underwater capabilities, potentially altering the strategic balance in the eastern Indian Ocean after over five decades.

title: Dell CEO Michael Dell makes one of largest public university donations in US history
content: Michael Dell has gifted a staggering $750 million to the University of Texas at Austin, propelling the creation of a groundbreaking AI-native hospital and research campus. This substantial donation, adding to his family's legacy of support, will also bolster scholarships, advanced computing, and vital medical research, underscoring Dell's enduring connection to his alma mater.

title: Magnus Carlsen suffers another Indian setback as Arjun Erigaisi stuns world No. 1
content: Indian Grandmaster Arjun Erigaisi stunned World Champion Magnus Carlsen at the FIDE World Rapid and Blitz Team Championships. Erigaisi capitalized on a late blunder by Carlsen to secure a crucial victory for Team MGD1. This win highlights the rising prowess of India's young chess talents, with Arjun joining the ranks of players challenging the world's best.

title: 'Chhatron Ki Goonj': Cong launches platform for students; Rahul shares video
content: In a passionate call to action, Rahul Gandhi invited students and aspiring professionals to support the 'Chhatron Ki Goonj' initiative by signing an online petition. This campaign targets the pressing issues of exam paper leaks, educational challenges, soaring tuition fees, and the alarming rate of unemployment.

title: 5 men gang-rape woman; doctors remove bullet, stone and wood from private part
content: A woman in Begusarai, Bihar, was allegedly gang-raped by five men who broke into her home. The attackers reportedly locked her husband away before assaulting her, leaving her with severe injuries. Doctors later removed a bullet, stone, and wood from her private part.

title: JPMorgan Chase & Goldman Sachs send 'Anthropic ban' message to employees outside US
content: JPMorgan Chase and Goldman Sachs have restricted their Hong Kong staff from using Anthropic's AI models, citing usage terms and potential national security concerns. This follows a US government order to Anthropic to halt exports of certain AI models globally. European nations are alarmed by these restrictions, pushing for independent AI development.

title: Meet football fan who stands like a statue for 90 minutes
content: DR Congo's iconic 'statue fan', Lumumba Vea, missed their World Cup opener against Portugal due to Ebola quarantine. Famous for his motionless tribute to Patrice Lumumba, his absence was noted. Fortunately, he's expected back for their next match, a welcome sight for fans after the team's surprising 1-1 draw.

title: US-Iran ink MoU, real test begins now: A defiant Israel can still derail peace efforts
content: A new US-Iran deal, brokered by Pakistan, aims to end a costly war and reopen vital sea lanes. The 'Islamabad MOU' includes lifting sanctions and withdrawing US forces, while Iran reopens the Strait of Hormuz. However, analysts warn the pact is fragile, with Israel's non-participation posing a significant risk to its success and a lasting peace.

title: Harry Kane and Kate Goodland turned a childhood romance Into marriage
content: Harry Kane enters his third World Cup as England's captain and all-time top scorer, fresh off a 36-goal Bundesliga season with Bayern Munich. Away from the pitch, his wife Katie Goodland has been part of his story since childhood. The pair met at age four in Chingford, started dating as teenagers in 2011, and married in 2019. A qualified fitness coach with a Sports Science degree, Goodland now balances her own career with raising their four children while supporting Kane through England's latest push to end six decades of World Cup hurt.

title: Crissy Froyd claims Mike Silver liked old bikini photos after Russini Story Row
content: Crissy Froyd has accused The Athletic's Mike Silver of liking old bikini photos on her Instagram immediately after she published a column about the Mike Vrabel-Dianna Russini scandal, alleging the behaviour was targeted. Froyd, who was previously fired from USA Today Sports over her criticism of Russini, has alleged that at least six female reporters admitted to her that they traded intimacy for NFL access. Meanwhile, Russini has resigned from The Athletic, which is now conducting an internal investigation.

title: New claim about Jorge Messi’s health reframes Lionel Messi’s World Cup Tears
content: Lionel Messi revealed that his emotional reaction after Argentina’s World Cup win was unrelated to football. According to comments made by journalist Eduardo Feinmann on Radio Mitre, concerns about the health of Messi’s father, Jorge Messi, may have been behind the tears. Despite the personal situation, Messi starred in Argentina’s 3-0 victory over Algeria.

title: PM Modi pitches India as global technology and AI hub at VivaTech 2026 in Paris
content: At VivaTech 2026, Prime Minister Modi showcased India's technological innovation, proudly articulating the nation’s role as a frontrunner in AI development, robust digital frameworks, and an exciting startup ecosystem.

title: 'We made him a one-format player': Ex-India cricketer questions major selection call
content: Former India batter Aakash Chopra feels Mohammed Siraj was prematurely labelled a one-format player. Chopra argues Siraj has consistently proven his mettle across all formats, particularly in white-ball cricket, despite facing periods of being out of favour. He questioned decisions regarding Siraj's white-ball selections, especially his omission from the Champions Trophy squad.

title: Aerial warfare revolution: US moves to deploy robot wingmen; India developing CATS Warrior
content: The US Air Force is revolutionizing aerial warfare with its Collaborative Combat Aircraft (CCA) program, integrating unmanned drones with crewed fighters. This initiative, featuring General Atomics' FQ-42 and Anduril's FQ-44, aims to enhance reach and survivability.

title: Ben Stokes returns to action amid nightclub controversy as England await investigation outcome
content: England Test captain Ben Stokes is set to return to competitive cricket for Durham in their County Championship match against Northamptonshire. He was previously omitted from England's second Test against New Zealand due to a nightclub incident. Both Stokes and Gus Atkinson have now been cleared for domestic play, with a decision on their international availability expected soon.

title: Quote of the day for children by Stephen Hawking
content: Stephen Hawking's profound quote offers a philosophy for a fulfilling life, urging us to "look up at the stars" for wonder, "never give up work" for purpose, and "cherish love" for connection. This balanced approach, emphasizing aspiration, effort, and affection, provides a timeless guide to navigating life's challenges and finding meaning.

title: The defection season: How rebels are redrawing India's political map
content: Indian opposition parties are experiencing internal turmoil and potential splits.  The Trinamool Congress, Shiv Sena (UBT), Samajwadi Party, and Aam Aadmi Party are all facing significant challenges.  These developments are reshaping the political landscape and impacting parliamentary arithmetic.  The trend suggests a season of political breakups is underway, with more expected.

title: Indian funds in Swiss banks fall 8% to Rs 36.79 cr in 2025, but customer deposits climb 50%
content: Funds linked to Indian clients in Swiss banks saw an 8% decline in 2025, totaling 3.25 billion Swiss francs. Despite an overall drop, direct customer account deposits surged over 50%. This follows a significant rebound in 2024, with the SNB clarifying data doesn't represent black money.

title: When PM Modi didn't eat the food prepared by Chef Sanjeev Kapoor and his team
content: Celebrity chef Sanjeev Kapoor faced an unexpected challenge in Abu Dhabi when Prime Minister Narendra Modi was fasting, requiring a last-minute menu change. Initially planned elaborate dishes were replaced with a special vegetarian spread.  Despite the sudden shift, Kapoor and his team adapted, showcasing their culinary prowess and dedication to serving with a smile.

title: 1,000 drones & cruise missiles: Ukraine launches largest attack against Russia
content: Moscow faced its largest Ukrainian drone assault, with nearly 200 unmanned aerial vehicles targeting the capital, causing fires and flight disruptions. Russia claimed to have intercepted almost 1,000 drones nationwide. Kyiv framed the strikes as retaliation and a warning, highlighting its growing long-range capabilities and aiming to pressure Moscow towards diplomacy.

title: Why travellers are flocking to see Jonathan, the world’s oldest living land animal at 194
content: Jonathan, a Seychelles giant tortoise on Saint Helena, is recognized as the world's oldest-known land animal at approximately 194 years old. Nominated for a Guinness World Records Icons list, he predates many famous landmarks and offers a unique connection to history. Scientists study his DNA to understand aging, while locals cherish him as a beloved symbol of endurance.

title: Restaurant told to pay Rs 50,000 for stale food, last-minute venue change
content: A Thiruvananthapuram restaurant has been ordered to pay Rs 50,000 to a customer whose daughter's first birthday party was marred by poor service and stale food. The district consumer commission found the restaurant's manager and managing director jointly liable for deficiency in service, citing unrebutted allegations of empty food containers, absent staff, and complaints about the quality of dishes served.

title: Sand mining feud: BJP neta burnt alive in car, 2 more killed in midnight ambush in Chhatisgarh
content: A violent sand mining turf war in Chhattisgarh's Koriya district resulted in a triple murder. A BJP functionary was burnt alive in a luxury SUV, with two others dying from injuries after a suspected planned ambush. Police are investigating the escalating tensions between rival groups involved in the sand business.

title: FIFA World Cup 2026 matches today: Mexico vs South Korea, Canada vs Qatar
content: The FIFA World Cup 2026 enters its eighth day with crucial Group A and B clashes. Hosts Mexico face South Korea, while Canada seeks its first win against Qatar. Czechia and South Africa battle to revive their campaigns, and Switzerland meets Bosnia and Herzegovina in a vital Group B encounter.

title: Win an ICC title, earn 500% extra: Pakistan cricket's new reward plan for players
content: Pakistan's centrally contracted cricketers are set for substantial pay hikes under the new 2026-27 contract system, with Track A red-ball players to see the biggest increase. The PCB's format-based contracts will also offer attractive performance bonuses, including a 500% match fee bonus for winning an ICC tournament.

title: Escape tunnel, CCTVs, 'modern gurukul': How Pune 'godman' exploited woman for 15 yrs
content: Pune police arrested a self-styled godman and seven associates for allegedly sexually assaulting, torturing, and financially exploiting a woman for over 15 years. Radhamohan Mishra, operating a 'modern gurukul,' allegedly used divine claims and psychological manipulation to control followers. The victim was isolated, surveilled, and subjected to severe abuse before escaping.

title: 'Telegram now a new dark web': Govt defends ban on app ahead of NEET retest
content: In an affidavit filed in response to Telegram's plea challenging the government's move to temporarily restrict access to the app ahead of the June 21 NEET-UG retest, the government countered that the "scale of the said channel itself demonstrates the capability of Telegram to facilitate mass dissemination of unlawful examination-related content". "Telegram has become the new dark web, linking threat actors.

title: Sooryavanshi row: BCCI secretary breaks silence on Sri Lanka incident
content: BCCI secretary Devajit Saikia clarified the board's stance on the on-field altercation involving Vaibhav Sooryavanshi and Vishen Halambage. Saikia emphasised that disciplinary actions are solely the purview of match officials, not the BCCI. Sri Lanka Cricket has reportedly sanctioned Halambage for provocative remarks and Dickwella for excessive appealing following the Super Over match.

title: 'Op Tudwa' vs 'Op Tiger': Sanjay Raut claims 'rebel' MPs moved to Rajasthan
content: Shiv Sena (UBT) leader Sanjay Raut alleged dissident MPs received Rs 10 crore each and were moved to Rajasthan amid a party split. Six of nine Lok Sabha MPs skipped a crucial meeting, prompting Raut to initiate action for whip violation. Maharashtra minister Sanjay Shirsat, however, blamed Raut for the unrest and denied his party's involvement in 'Operation Tiger'.

title: 'He will do whatever job you ask': Ex-England spinner's massive praise for India's unsung hero
content: Graeme Swann lauded Ishan Kishan's exceptional century, a blistering 125 off 79 balls, which was instrumental in India's commanding 170-run victory over Afghanistan. Kishan's remarkable innings, featuring a rapid transition from circumspect play to aggressive stroke-making, showcased his adaptability and improved batting prowess. This performance, alongside Shubman Gill's 154, propelled India to a massive total and secured the series.

title: NEET paper leak: Lessons from India's biggest exam scandal
content: In light of the recent NEET-UG paper leak, India's examination infrastructure is under fire, revealing significant systemic weaknesses. The fallout from the CBSE's grading mishaps and Bihar's logistical challenges sheds light on ongoing issues that disrupt the process for countless hopeful candidates. As these scandals emerge, questions regarding equity, responsibility, and confidence in the examination framework become increasingly critical.

title: NFL Trade Rumors: Cowboys, Saints Chase Jets' $4.27M Running Back
content: Trade speculation around Isaiah Davis is starting to build as the New York Jets face a crowded backfield entering the 2026 season. With Breece Hall locked in as the lead option and Braelon Allen expected to handle a bigger role, insiders believe Davis could be expendable. The Dallas Cowboys and New Orleans Saints have emerged as logical landing spots, with both teams looking for added running back depth before training camp begins

title: Portugal needs to put 'GOAT' Ronaldo out to pasture
content: Is England actually good under a German? Will South Korea and Mexico bonhomie stop? What was the desi connection in Portugal vs Congo?

title: How Israel seized 1,000 sq km in two years, its biggest territorial expansion in decades
content: Israel has significantly expanded its military-held territory in Gaza, Lebanon, and Syria since October 2023, encompassing approximately 1,000 square kilometers. This move, described as creating "buffer zones," has displaced millions and raises concerns about permanence, despite not being recognized as new borders. This marks its biggest expansion of its militarily-held territories in years.

title: At Trump's Iran Deal Signing Venue, A 1919 Treaty Left This Nation Red-Faced
content: The historic palace, sprawling across more than 800 hectares to the west of Paris, has long served as a backdrop for the world's most consequential negotiations.

title: Trump Tore Up Obama-Era Iran Deal. How It Compares To 2026 Memorandum
content: On Wednesday, the US and Iran signed the Islamabad Memorandum of Understanding, a 14-point framework aimed at ending a conflict in the Middle East that had stretched to around 110 days.

title: Rockstar Unveils GTA 6 Cover Art, Announces Pre-Order Date
content: Set to release on November 19, 2026, GTA 6 arrives more than 13 years after Grand Theft Auto V, the second best-selling video game of all time, and expectations could hardly be higher.

title: Cross-Voting In Jharkhand Powers BJP-Backed Candidate To Rajya Sabha
content: A BJP-backed candidate won a seat in Jharkhand's Rajya Sabha election today, after cross-voting by MLAs of the CPI(ML) and Lalu Yadav's Rashtriya Janata Dal, sources said.

title: Why Punjab Congress Feels Caught In A 2021 Time Warp
content: In 2021, the Congress high command found itself grappling with an escalating feud between then Chief Minister Captain Amarinder Singh and state party chief Navjot Singh Sidhu. Five years later, the party appears to be following a similar roadmap.

title: "Faith Meets Fitness": Muslim Girls Along With Hindu Friends Perform Yoga
content: At a time when some choose to view every act through a communal lens, a breathtaking frame emerges from Jammu and Kashmir - one that narrates the real story of Bharat.

title: Alleged Bomb Attack On BJP Workers In Bengal's Karimpur, Five Injured
content: According to local sources, the attack took place in the Sahebpara area under Karimpur-II Block while BJP workers were returning home

title: Rare, Under-Studied Goat-Antelope Spotted In Sikkim After Nearly 3 Decades
content: Mishmi Takin: During a routine patrolling exercise conducted in Bakuchen under Tingda Reserve Forest, forest officials recorded the first-ever video footage of the species from the area.

title: Bihar Horror: Woman Gang-Raped, Bullet Inserted In Private Parts, Cuts On Body
content: The survivor said the attackers took her to a desolate place outside her house and raped her. When she tried to raise an alarm, they sliced her chest and thigh with a blade.

title: RBI Approves Extension Of Keki Mistry's Tenure As HDFC Bank Interim Chairman
content: HDFC Bank on Thursday said the Reserve Bank has approved the extension of Keki Mistry's tenure as interim chairman for three months till September 18.

title: Team Uddhav Rebels Were Upset. A Sanjay Raut Remark Made It Worse
content: There has been no official response from Nagesh Aashtikar, Sanjay Deshmukh, Sanjay Jadhav, Sanjay Dina Patil, Omprakash Rajenimbalkar and Bhausaheb Wakchaure, who had skipped the meeting.

title: Vietnamese Tycoon's Seized Hermes Collection Sold For Over Rs 5 Crore
content: The sale included several Hermes Birkin bags, which are among the most expensive and in-demand luxury handbags in the world.

title: Chennai Prodigy Lydian Nadhaswaram Makes History With London Orchestra
content: The symphony was recorded with the London Symphony Orchestra under the baton of acclaimed conductor Matt Dunkley.

title: Telegram Is "Like Frankenstein": Centre Defends Move To Block Platform
content: "Telegram has become the new dark web... Criminals have rapidly adopted Telegram to post links on channels that connect to dark web forums," the Centre told the HIgh Court today

title: After PM Modi-Trump G7 Meet, Amit Shah Holds Security Talks With US Envoy
content: He said under the leadership of Prime Minister Modi, India is firmly committed to advancing the India-U.S. Comprehensive Global Strategic Partnership and ensuring that the people of both nations benefit from the bilateral relationship.

title: French Tanker Passes Strait Of Hormuz After War Deal: Report
content: A French-flagged LNG tanker left the Gulf through the Strait of Hormuz on Thursday, a tracking platform said, as an agreement to end the US-Iran war paved the way for the key passage's reopening.

title: Assam Prepares For Ambubachi Mela, Over 8 Lakh Devotees Expected
content: The festival will commence with Pravritti on June 22 and conclude with Nivritti on June 26, when the temple doors reopen after remaining closed for three days.

title: Video: Ukrainian Drone Hits Russian Refinery, Blows Tank Lid Off
content: According to Russian officials, Ukraine launched one of its biggest drone attacks on Moscow in more than two years.

title: Telangana’s EAGLE force tracks drug smugglers through Odisha forests, seizes 500 kg marijuana
content: Acting on specific intelligence, a patrol team led by Sub-Inspector of Police K. Ravi Prasad tracked the vehicle for nearly 80 km in the night-long pursuit

title: ‘Kafir’ screenshot case puts CPI(M) on the defensive again
content: The UDF has seized on the development to intensify its attack on the CPI(M), arguing that the ‘Kafir’ screenshot case is exposing the party’s “real face”

title: Kerala Chief Minister V.D. Satheesan to present Revised Budget for 2026-27 on June 19, 2026
content: The  presentation of the Budget, the first by the new Congress-led United Democratic Front (UDF) government, will begin at 9 a.m.

title: Dengue cases on the rise in Kerala
content: Health officials say the pattern of intermittent rainfall followed by warm spells may lead to an explosion in vector indices in the coming weeks. Dengue has claimed 17 lives in 2026 and another 15 deaths are also considered as probable dengue deaths

title: ED arrests former AP Minister’s son in liquor transport scam probe
content: The Central agency is investigating alleged irregularities in liquor transportation contracts awarded by the Andhra Pradesh State Beverages Corporation Limited

title: Kodagu DC weighs tourist safety before reopening glass bridge attractions
content: At the glass bridge near the junction leading to Abbey Falls and Mandalpatti, the DC examined the permissions obtained for the project, engineering certification of the structure, parking facilities, visitor management arrangements and the fee charged to tourists
